summary.authoritative_rule: the intraday add branch is now research-complete enough at the supervision layer: its local hierarchy is rich and internally consistent, but broader positive portability, observed single-slot fallback, and execution authority remain blocked

component_rows.reading: the add branch owns a stable seed registry and no longer lacks first-layer supervision objects
current local add rules fully recover the seed surface and preserve the supervision ladder
shape-only positive add promotion remains too dense on the wider local session surface
the branch has a believable local permission family, but it remains local rather than portable
persistent confirmation is clean locally, but still not a broader promotion license
slot capacity now has a usable local hierarchy: zero-slot veto or tiered dual-slot
the branch still does not own a naturally observed single-slot fallback template
the frontier has grown a rich supervision stack, but it still lacks portable permission and execution-safe allocation modules

interpretation: V1.34GN stops asking for another local clue and instead asks whether the add branch has crossed from open exploration into supervision-level completion.

The answer is yes at the supervision layer. The branch now owns registry, seed rules, local permission hierarchy, and local slot-capacity hierarchy. What remains missing is not more local semantics, but portable promotion and execution authority.
